metadata.title: Switching High-Current Battery Power with a MOSFET
metadata.summary: Why a normal switch can't handle the power a drone battery dumps at start-up, and how a MOSFET — bolted to an aluminium heatsink — solves it.

image.alt: Abstract render of a MOSFET power switching circuit

metadata.author: Louis Place

data.content: The battery will release a lot of energy for the drone when it powers on. This energy is complicated to handle for a normal switch, which is why we need a **MOSFET** transistor to handle the power switching. This kind of transistor only requires a small amount of power to switch, instead of the large amount of power needed by a standard switch.

The first thing to do is cut a piece of aluminium plate, since I expect a lot of heat release. Even a good MOSFET has a tiny bit of resistance when it's *on*, and at high current it still makes some heat — so the transistor bolts to the aluminium, which acts as a heatsink.
